title: AI Trust & Safety Digest
tagline: A curated current snapshot of AI trust, safety, and governance, including as many materially important fresh items as the day warrants.
lead: Today’s signal is concentrated in evaluation quality, alignment measurement, and deploy-time control rather than major policy moves. The strongest updates focus on how to better measure model behavior, improve benchmark reliability, and add safety controls or compliance structure around generative systems and sensitive data workflows.

items.title: Google proposes evaluating LLM alignment through behavioral dispositions
items.why_it_matters: It pushes alignment assessment beyond single answers toward more stable behavioral tendencies.

items.title: Google examines how many raters are needed for reliable AI benchmarks
items.why_it_matters: Benchmark reliability directly affects safety claims, model comparisons, and deployment decisions.

items.title: Modular Energy Steering proposes inference-time safety control for text-to-image models
items.why_it_matters: Inference-time steering could add adaptable safeguards without retraining foundation models.

items.title: De Jure uses iterative self-refinement to extract regulatory rules from legal texts
items.why_it_matters: Structured rule extraction could make compliance requirements more machine-readable and auditable.

items.title: Bayesian vertical federated learning paper targets privacy-preserving multimodal survival modeling
items.why_it_matters: It addresses privacy and uncertainty for sensitive multi-party model training on distributed data.

items.title: User-turn generation is proposed as a probe of interaction awareness in language models
items.why_it_matters: It offers a new way to test whether models understand downstream conversational effects.

items.title: Memory forgetting techniques for autonomous agents aim to reduce false memory propagation
items.why_it_matters: Memory management is a practical safety issue for long-horizon agents and persistent assistants.

note: Selected from recent trust-and-safety relevant posts and papers by labs, evaluators, standards bodies, policy organizations, and arXiv, then summarized with GPT-5.4. Includes as many materially important fresh items as the day warrants.
